Simplify gene set results via ontologies

simplify_hypergeom_res.Rd

This function provides an interface to simplify overenrichment results based on ontological information of the pathway origin (typical use case is to simplify gene ontology results). To do so, the function will calculate the Wang similarity and keep within a set of highly similar terms the one with the lowest fdr. Should there be terms with the same fdr, the function will keep the most specific term within the ontology.

Usage

simplify_hypergeom_res(res, parent_child_dt, weights, min_sim = 0.7)

Arguments

res

data.table. The enrichment results. Needs to have the columns c("gene_set_name", "fdr").

parent_child_dt

data.table. The data.table with column parent and child. You also need to have a type column for the Wang similarity to provide the weights for the relationships.

weights

Named numeric. The relationship of type to weight for this specific edge. For example c("part_of" = 0.8, "is_a" = 0.6).

min_sim

Float between 0 and 1. The minimum similarity that the terms need to have.

Value

data.table with enrichment results.

On this page
